description: In an apartment living room and kitchen area, Monica lowers a cordless phone while Chandler and Rachel stand nearby.
Monica holds out the phone and says, "He had to go. There was a deer just outside eating fruit from the orchard."
Rachel scoffs, looking at Monica.
Rachel gestures with her hands as she says, "I don't get this. She was horrible." Chandler points his thumbs over his shoulders, adds, "Okay, I'm going to go stand over there," and walks away.
Rachel watches Chandler walk toward the living area, then turns her gaze back to Monica.
Monica asks, "Why do you care so much anyway?"
Rachel places her hands on her hips and says, "I don't care."
Rachel raises both hands to her hair and exhales audibly.
Moving her hands outward, Rachel says, "You know what? I'm just upset that I'm getting nowhere with Joshua. You know what? Still, you do not just"
Rachel points her index finger forward as she finishes, "meet someone and go flitting off to Vermont."
Monica shifts her posture, looking directly at Rachel.

Monica begins, "When you first met Barry, you flitted off to," before Rachel interrupts, raising her hands and saying, "know could you just for once not remember every little," as she turns and walks away.
Rachel walks past Chandler toward the hallway. Chandler turns to face Monica.
Chandler points his finger and asks Monica, "you know when's he getting back?"

Monica answers, "A couple of days." Chandler says, "You know what? I knew something like this was going to happen," while unfastening and pulling down his pants to reveal sweatpants. Monica asks, "What are you doing?"
Chandler steps entirely out of his pants.
Monica says, "Chandler, you can't just go back a phase." Chandler drops into an armchair, waves his hand, and says, "Yes, you can. You're thinking about time. You can't go back in time."
Phoebe walks in from the hallway and approaches Chandler's chair.
Phoebe leans over the back of the chair and says, "Well, look, why don't you just why don't you do your phase two strip club thing with us?"
Monica steps forward and says, "Yeah, come on. We can be guys." Chandler shakes his head and replies, "No, you can't."
Phoebe stands upright, stepping slightly away from the chair.
Phoebe says, "Come on, let us be guys. Maybe we want to be guys." Chandler stands up from the chair, replying, "You don't want to be guys."
Gesturing with his hands, Chandler continues, "You'd be all hairy, and you wouldn't live as long."
Phoebe steps closer to Chandler.
Phoebe points at Chandler, her volume rising as she says, "You just stop being such a wuss and get those off. And you come with us and watch naked girls dance around."
Chandler stares at Phoebe in silence, his mouth slightly open.
Chandler says softly, "Okay," lowering his gaze.
Phoebe looks at him, lowering her hands.
Phoebe embraces Chandler and says, "I'm sorry."
The scene changes to the exterior of an awning reading "Silvercup Studios," then to an interior set where A.D. stands holding a clipboard. Joey runs up to him.
Joey points to his own chest and says, "Joey Tribbiani. I'm here. I'm here. Calm down."
A.D. looks at his clipboard and replies, "We got time. Running a little late."
Charlton Heston walks past them. Joey turns his head to watch him approach a craft services table.
Heston picks up a red licorice whip and puts it in his mouth. Joey turns his attention back to A.D.
Joey points his thumb back toward Heston and asks, "Charlton Heston eating a licorice whip?"
A.D. turns his head slightly.
A.D. nods and says, "Yeah, he loves them." Joey looks off-screen and begins, "I've never seen him."
Joey stops mid-sentence, turns back to A.D., and audibly sniffs the air.
Joey leans his head back slightly and says, "Whoa."
Joey asks A.D., "Yeah. What the hell is that? What smells so bad?"
A.D. looks directly at Joey and answers, "You."
Joey's eyes widen, his mouth dropping open slightly.
Joey gestures with one hand and replies, "I could see why you would think that. But"
Joey lowers his hand and shifts his gaze.
Joey points his finger slightly and continues, "actually, you know who I think it?"
A.D. looks at him and asks, "You?"
Joey shakes his head side to side.
Joey says, "No, it's Heston," pointing a thumb over his shoulder.
In the background, Heston stands reading a script with another man. A.D. furrows his brow.
A.D. asks, "What?" Joey replies, "Oh, the man reeks. Sounds like he went on a three day fishing trip and then ate some licorice."
A.D. shakes his head.
A.D. says, "There's no way he smells. He's the only one around here with a shower in his dressing."
Joey nods slowly, his eyes widening again.
Joey leans in and asks, "Shower, huh? And which room might that be?" A.D. replies, "The one with Heston on it."
Joey looks away, nodding his head.
Joey says, "Interesting," while maintaining a slight nod.
The scene changes to a daytime view of a city street with traffic. A neon sign reading "Topless Go-Go Girls" flashes.
Dance music plays. Inside a dimly lit club with pink lighting, a woman dances on a stage near a pole.
Dance music continues. Chandler, Monica, and Phoebe sit at a table in the club with drinks. Monica stands up holding cash and leans toward Chandler.
Monica holds up the cash and asks, "I got some ones. You want to put them in her panties?"
Chandler looks at Monica and replies, "No, thanks, mom."
Chandler rests his chin on his hand.
In a dimly lit club with pink lighting, Phoebe sits next to Cigarette Guy, who holds a lit cigarette.
Phoebe turns her head toward Cigarette Guy and says, "Oh, no, hi. That you have to put that out because I'm pregnant."
Cigarette Guy takes a drag from his cigarette and replies, "Maybe you and your baby ought to go to another"
Cigarette Guy continues, "strip club."
Phoebe points her finger at the man.
Phoebe says, "It's not my baby," as Cigarette Guy exhales smoke.
Chandler and Monica sit at a table while a dancer in silver shorts performs nearby. Monica holds cash and leans toward the dancer, and Chandler claps his hands.
Monica slides the cash into the dancer's waistband and says, "Very good."
The dancer walks away. Rachel walks toward the table.
Monica looks at the departing dancer and says, "Really enjoyed it. Very exotic."
Rachel places her bag on the table and sits down.
Rachel says, "Well, I just checked our messages, and Joshua didn't call. But you would think he would be worried about me not showing up at his"
Rachel says, "club. And you know what makes it so much worse?"
A dancer steps onto the nearby stage as Rachel continues, "Ross is all happy and Vermont. Come on, look where you"
Rachel points toward the dancer on the stage.
A waitress in a red outfit approaches the table. Rachel finishes her sentence, saying, "sick."
Monica turns to the waitress and says, "Another round of daiquiris."
Phoebe says, "Remember, virgin for me, please." Monica adds, "Oh, and don't let me leave without getting the name of that carpet."
The waitress stands by the table.

Rachel gestures to her own chest and says to the waitress, "And Sharon, you know what, hon? You might want to just oh, come on." The waitress adjusts her top, and Chandler rubs his face with his hands.
The waitress walks away.
Chandler waves his hands and says, "You know what? You know what? I'm just going to go home and call Kathy."
Phoebe asks Chandler, "You think it'll help?"
Chandler lowers his hands to the table.
Chandler replies, "No, that was a test."
Chandler leans forward slightly.
Chandler waves his hands and says, "In a couple hours, I'm going to get really drunk and I'm going to want to call Kathy. And you guys are going to have to stop"
Chandler continues, "me. And then after that, I'm going to be so drunk I'm going to want to call Janice." Phoebe smiles and says, "You should. How is she?"
Monica looks at Chandler.
Monica says, "I think somebody needs another lap dance," then stands up and waves her hands toward someone off-screen.
Chandler plugs his ears as a dancer approaches the table.
The scene changes to the exterior of a brick building with a sign reading "SILVERCUP STUDIOS." The setting then changes to a dressing room, where water runs audibly behind a closed shower curtain.
Heston, wearing a shoulder holster over a white shirt, opens the door and walks into the dressing room.
Heston looks toward the shower and walks closer, closing the door behind him.
Heston knocks on the wall near the shower, resting one hand on his hip, and grabs the edge of the curtain.
Heston pulls back the curtain, revealing Joey standing inside the shower under the running water, and says, "there's."
Joey turns to face Heston.
Joey says, "How you doing?"
Heston looks at Joey.
Heston says, "Who the hell are you?"
Joey smiles slightly.
Joey says, "I guess you wouldn't believe me if I said I was Douglas, huh?"
Heston stares at Joey without changing his facial expression.
Heston points a finger at Joey and says, "Put some pants on, kid, so I can kick your"
Joey raises his hands in front of his chest.
Joey gestures with his hands and says, "no, no, wait. You don't understand. See, I'm an actor."
Joey gestures to himself.
Joey says, "Joey Tribbiani. I'm doing a scene with you today, and well, I stink."
Heston looks at Joey.
Heston asks, "You are in this?" and Joey quickly replies, "Yeah, yeah."
Joey says, "I'm one of the cops who won't work with you because you're a loose cannon. Anyway, look, I'm really sorry. But I stink."
Heston lowers his head slightly.
Heston looks back up and says, "Joey, right."
Joey nods and replies, "Yeah."
Heston turns and walks to the couch. Joey steps out of the shower and wraps a towel around his waist.
Heston sits down and says, "Every actor, one time or another up."
Joey walks over and sits on the edge of the coffee table across from Heston.
Heston says, "Every actor thinks he stinks. Even Lawrence Olivier sometimes thought he Stank. Bob Redford won't even"
Heston finishes, "watch." Joey stands up, clutching his towel, and says, "No, no, you don't understand. Listen to me."
Heston looks up at Joey and says, "Yeah?"
Heston stands up from the couch.
Heston says, "I don't know one actor worthy salt who one time in his career didn't say, God, I"
Heston says, "stink. Hell, I just did a scene out there. First take, I stunk the place up."
Joey looks at Heston with his arms crossed.
Heston leans forward and says, "But the important thing that you must remember no matter how badly you think you"
Heston steps closer, moving his face close to Joey's.
Heston points at Joey and says, "stink you must never, ever bust into my dressing room and use my shower."
Joey stares back with widened eyes.
Heston asks, "Do you understand me?" Joey replies, "Yes, sir."
Joey turns toward the door.
Heston says, "Wait a minute. Take your pants." Joey replies, "Yeah."
Joey scoops up a pile of clothes and boots from the couch.
Holding his clothes, Joey moves quickly toward the door and says, "Sorry. I'm really sorry."
Joey steps quickly out of the dressing room, leaving the door open. Heston watches him leave.
Heston sighs and says, "What a business."
Heston sits down on the couch as the segment ends.
Inside an apartment, Monica and Chandler walk through the open front door into the kitchen area. Monica wears a black coat with a fur collar, and Chandler wears a dark jacket and scarf.
Monica turns to Chandler and asks, "So we did okay at the strip club, right?"
Chandler replies, "Oh, yeah, that was great." Phoebe is visible in the background removing her red coat.
Chandler nods slightly.
Chandler says, "Thanks to you, the hottest cocktail waitress there is quitting to teach the third grade."
Rachel enters through a doorway, wearing a black coat over a light blue top and jeans.
Rachel says, "I can't believe it."
Rachel continues, "He still hasn't called."
Phoebe asks, "Who, Josh?" Rachel replies, "It's Joshua." Audience laughter is audible. Phoebe says, "What? He isn't like Josh." Rachel responds, "No, I don't."
Chandler grabs his jacket from Monica.
Chandler says, "Right. Well, I'm going to put my sweats back on," and turns toward the hallway. Phoebe steps into his path.
Phoebe extends her arm, blocking his path, and says, "No, wait. Okay, you know what? You were right. You were right. We really weren't"
Phoebe continues, "great at being guys. But do you know why? Because we're girls." Chandler looks at her.
Chandler stares at Phoebe without speaking.
Chandler says, "Yeah, okay." Phoebe asks, "Do you know what girls are really good at?" Chandler replies, "Stripping," prompting audience laughter.
Phoebe shakes her head and gestures toward the nearby recliner.
"No, listening. Sit," Phoebe says, grabbing Chandler's arm and pushing him down into the recliner as audience laughter plays.
Monica and Phoebe kneel on either side of Chandler's chair while Rachel stands behind it.
Kneeling beside him, Phoebe says, "Maybe just it would really help if you would just talk."
Monica adjusts Chandler's shirt collar and says, "Yeah, come on. What's going on in there?"
Chandler looks back and forth between the three women surrounding him.
Monica says, "You know, if you want to cry, that's okay too." Chandler keeps his hands in his lap. Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler raises his hands slightly and says, "Okay, look, I'm going to have to ask you all to leave."
Rachel leans forward over the back of the chair.
Rachel says, "Chandler," and Chandler replies, "look, forget it. We tried, but phase three is a lost cause, okay?"
Chandler continues, "Those strippers were insanely hot," gesturing with one hand.
Chandler covers his face with his hands and finishes, "and I couldn't picture myself with any of them," followed by audience laughter.
Chandler keeps his hands over his face, leaning his head back. The three women look at him and then at each other.
Rachel says, "They really were pretty, weren't know?" Phoebe says, "I really like that fighter pilot one." Monica adds, "Oh, Candy?"
Monica says, "Oh, she was so"
Phoebe nods and smiles.
Rachel says, "but you know, I think if I were going to be with a woman"
Chandler lowers his hands and looks up at Rachel.
Rachel says, "it'd be with someone like Michelle. Because she was just so petite."
Monica replies, "See, I don't know. For me, it would have to be"
Phoebe speaks briefly, and Chandler's eyes widen.
An uncertain speaker says, "know." Chandler says, "Oh, my God."
Monica gestures with her hands as she speaks.
Monica says, "She had the smoothest skin. I mean, when I stuck that dollar bill in her G string and grazed her thigh"
Chandler stands up quickly from the chair and shouts, "phase three." Audience laughter begins.
Chandler turns to look at the women.
Chandler says, "I just achieved phase three," pacing slightly.
"Really?" Monica asks.
Chandler gestures wildly and says, "I am totally picturing you with all those women."
The three women stand up and stare at him with wide eyes and open mouths as audience laughter plays.
Monica says, "That's not phase three."
Chandler points at himself and says, "I'm there too."
The women grimace, pulling their heads back in revulsion.
Rachel asks, "Well, are we all together? Like in a group?" Chandler replies, "Stop it," over audience laughter.
Chandler says, "You're killing me," holding his hands up.
Chandler gestures emphatically.
Chandler says, "I think I just moved on to phase four."
Rachel asks, "What is that?" Chandler replies, "What is that where I don't want to have a relationship ever."
Chandler continues, "I just want to have sex with strippers and my friends," prompting loud audience laughter.
The women stare at Chandler. The setting changes to a daytime exterior of a city street, then to the inside of a coffee shop where Monica, Chandler, Joey, and Phoebe sit around a coffee table.
Joey leans forward toward Chandler and says, "Come on, let me see that smile."
Chandler's gaze lowers to the table.
"I don't wanna," Chandler says softly.
Joey nudges his shoulder.
"Please?" Joey asks.
Chandler keeps his gaze down.
Joey says, "I wanted to go to the strip club."
Chandler turns his head toward Joey.
Chandler says, "You're gonna have plenty of chances, okay?"
Chandler gestures with his hands and adds, "There are, like, thousands of women out there who are just waiting to screw me over."
Joey smiles and pats Chandler's leg. Ross walks into the coffee shop and approaches their group.
Ross says, "Hey."
Monica looks up at Ross and asks, "Oh. How is Vermont?"
Ross looks around the group.
"Emily is," Ross says.
Ross pauses.
"incredible," Ross finishes.
Ross says, "I mean, there are no words to describe her."
Ross takes off his jacket and says, "The whole weekend was like a dream."
Rachel approaches the group from behind Ross.
Ross points at Rachel and says, "Oh, and you."
"Rach," Ross says.
Ross says, "Hey. You were so right."
Rachel looks at him with a tight jaw.
"What?" Rachel asks. Ross replies, "What you said about us finally being in a place"
Ross continues, "where we could really be happy for each I"
Ross shakes his head slightly.
Ross says, "i admit I wasn't quite there."
Ross looks closely at Rachel.
Ross says, "The thought of you and that Josh guy," and Rachel interrupts, "joshua."
Ross says, "Joshua guy at that club, dancing, having a good time"
Ross gestures as he continues, "and the thought of it kind"
Ross looks at Rachel, who stares back tightly, as the segment ends.
In a coffee shop, Ross stands facing Rachel.
Ross gestures with his hands as he says, "now, but now I'm totally there. I'm finally where you are."
Rachel clasps her hands together, smiles, and replies, "Oh, thank"
Rachel finishes, "goodness."
Rachel tilts her head.
Ross nods and says, "And thank you for Emily."
Rachel taps his arm and replies, "Oh, no problem. I'm so glad I could know. Happy for"
Ross smiles and says, "you. Happy for you."
Rachel smiles back at him.
Rachel says, "Happy for you," pats his arm again, and turns away. Audience laughter begins.

Rachel walks away, and Ross crosses his arms as audience laughter continues. The image turns black, then the setting changes to an apartment interior. Chandler stands in the kitchen area wearing a robe, surrounded by three women in bikinis.
Chandler points at the women and says, "All right, ladies, here's what we're going to do."
Chandler points to one of the women and says, "You are going to take off my clothes." Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler turns his attention to the other two women.

Chandler points to the other two women, then points at a third, raising his arms. He says, "You two go get the oils. And you just constantly scream at the top of your voice Chandler's the king. Chandler's the king," his volume rising on the final phrases, accompanied by audience laughter.
Phoebe sits on a nearby couch with the women in bikinis standing around her. She raises a finger, pointing toward one of the women.
Phoebe says, "I want to be with her. I like her." Chandler waves his hand and says, "Oh, that's fine. Go with your instincts. Go with your instincts." Audience laughter plays throughout the exchange.
Monica, sitting next to Rachel near the couch, looks up and asks, "What am I doing again?" Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler leans forward, his voice louder as he says, "Oh, come on. Would you please pay attention? I could wake up at any moment," followed by audience laughter.

A bald man in a suit, Cigarette Guy, holding an unlit cigarette, approaches Chandler and says, "Hi, I'm Joshua. I'm here to pick up". Rachel points at him and interrupts, "no, no, that's not Joshua." Audience laughter follows.
Cigarette Guy stops next to Chandler.
Chandler gestures with open hands towards Rachel and asks, "What do you want from me?"
Chandler lowers his arms and continues, "I never met the guy." Audience laughter is heard.
Chandler points towards Rachel.
Chandler gestures widely and says, "So anyway, Rachel, I'm sorry you can can't stay. The rest of us have a lot of work to do," followed by audience laughter.
Chandler puts his arm around Cigarette Guy's shoulder. Cigarette Guy places the cigarette in his mouth.
Chandler turns to Cigarette Guy and asks, "Wait, what are you doing?" Audience laughter plays.
Cigarette Guy keeps the cigarette in his mouth and looks at Chandler.

Chandler gestures with both hands and says, "All right, listen, I got to wake up." Cigarette Guy removes the cigarette from his mouth, and audience laughter is heard as upbeat instrumental music begins.
The image turns completely black while the instrumental music continues.
The image remains black, and the instrumental music plays until the segment ends.
